semanticCore: Glance, flash of light, wink, twinkling of an eye, instant, or look
primaryMeaning: Twinkling of an eye/flash of light
quranicRelevance: Hour matter like twinkling of eye (Lamh - 'ka-lamhi al-basari').

keyDerivations.meaning: Twinkling of an eye, flash of light, instant glance, wink, or briefest moment

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمَا أَمْرُ السَّاعَةِ إِلَّا كَلَمْحِ الْبَصَرِ أَوْ هُوَ أَقْرَبُ (16:77): And the matter of the Hour is not but like the twinkling of an eye/glance, or even closer (the speed of divine command execution).
discardedNoise: Hinting at a topic (Talmeeh) in literature books.
summaryEn: The root represents a twinkling instant (Lamh), describing the lightning-fast speed of the eschatological shift and the divine execution of decrees.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر اللمعان الخاطف السريع للضوء وطرفة العين (لمح)، معبراً عن سرعة وقوع القيامة ونفوذ المشيئة الإلهية كلمح البصر السريع الخاطف.
rawText: أَلْتُمِحَ بَصَرُهُ 8 His sight was taken away. (K.) === See 1. أَلْمَحِىٌّ A man (TA) who glances much, or frequently takes light, or slight looks. (K.) المحهُ 4 He made him to glance, or to take a light, or slight, look; expl. by جَعَلَهُ يَلْمَحَ, (K,) and جَعَلَهُ مِمَّنْ يَلْمَحُ. (TA.) === See 1. === أَلْمَحَتِ المَرْأَةُ مِنْ وَجْهِهَا, (inf. n. إِلْمَاحٌ, TA,) The woman allowed herself [or her face] to be glanced at, or to be viewed lightly, or slightly: so does a beautiful woman, displaying, and then concealing, her beauties. (T, K.) فُلَانٌ سَمْحٌ لَمْحٌ , and سَمِيحٌ 🡻لَمِيحٌ: see art. سمح. [لَمْحٌ and لَمِيحٌ in these cases seem to be merely imitative sequents.] === لَأُرِيَنَّكَ لَمْحًا بَاصِرًا ‡ I will assuredly show thee a manifest, or an evident, thing, matter, or affair. (S, A, K.) [See also art. بصر.] لَامَحَهُ 3 , inf. n. مُلَامَحَةٌ, [He glanced at him, or viewed him lightly, or slightly, or snatched sights of him unawares, reciprocally]. (A.) لَامِحٌ and 🡻لَمُوحٌ and 🡻لَمَّاحٌ Lightning, and a star, shining; gleaming; glistening: (K:) [but the second and third are intensive epithets, signifying shining much; &c.] === أَبْيَضُ 🡻لَمَّاحٌ ‡ Intensely white. (A.) === لَامِحُ عِطْفَيْهِ A self-admiring man, who looks at his sides. (M, F.) لَمَحَهُ 1 , (S,) or لَمَحَ إِلَيْهِ, aor. ﹷ, (L, Msb, K), inf. n. لَمْحٌ; (Msb;) and 🡻المح, (L, K,) or 🡻المحهُ, (S, Msb,) and 🡻التمحهُ; (S;) He glanced, or took a light, or slight, look, at him or it; (S;) like لَمَأَهُ; (Nh;) snatched a sight of him or it unawares: (L, Msb, K;) or, accord. to some, لَمَحَ signifies he looked; and 🡻المحهُ, he made him to look; but the former explanation is the more correct: or لَمَحَ is only said of one looking from a distance. (L.) === لَمَحْتُهُ بِالبَصَرِ I directed (صَوَّبْتُ) my sight towards him. (Msb.) === لَمَحَ البَصَرُ The sight extended to a thing. (Msb.) === لَمَحَ, aor. ﹷ, inf. n. لَمْحٌ (S, K) and لَمَحَانٌ and تَلْمَاحٌ, (K,) It (lightning, and a star,) shone; gleamed; glistened; i. q. لَمَعَ. (S, K.) لَمُوحٌ : see لَامِحٌ. لَمِيحٌ : see لَمْحٌ. لَمَّاحٌ : see لَامِحٌ. لَمْحَةٌ , subst. from لَمَحَ, (S, L,) A glance, or light or slight look. (L.) === لَمْحَةٌ A shining, gleaming, or glistening, of lightning. (S.) === فِى فُلَانٍ لَمْحَةٌ مِنْ أَبِيهِ In such a one is a likeness, or point of resemblance, to his father: then they said 🡻مَلامِحُمِنْ ابيه, (S,) signifying likenessess, or points of resemblance; forming an extr. pl., (S, K,) as though from another word than لَمْحَةٌ: (S:) they did not say مَلْمَحَةٌ. (TA.) === 🡻مَلَامِحُ also signifies What appear of the beauties and defects of the face (K) of a human being: or the parts thereof that are glanced at, or viewed lightly or quickly. (TA.) لُمَّاحٌ Sharp hawks; syn. صُقُورٌ ذَكِيَّةٌ. (IAar, T, K, [in the CK, زَكِيَّة].) مَلَامِحُ : see لَمْحَةٌ.
